clusters.title: Man jailed for unprovoked attack that killed elderly postmaster
clusters.neutral_headline: Man sentenced for unprovoked attack

items.title: Man jailed for unprovoked attack that killed elderly postmaster
items.standfirst: Troy Maskell is sentenced to seven years and nine months' prison after a retrial found him guilty a second time of manslaughter over the death of regional Victorian postmaster John Burke.
items.content: Troy Maskell is sentenced to seven years and nine months' prison after a retrial found him guilty a second time of manslaughter over the death of regional Victorian postmaster John Burke.

items.title: Twice-guilty man gets shorter sentence over death of beloved postmaster
items.standfirst: Troy Maskell has been sentenced to seven years and nine months over the deadly assault of Strathmerton postmaster John Burke.
items.content: A man who was twice found guilty of manslaughter over his drunken unprovoked assault on a beloved postmaster has been handed a shorter sentence the second time around.Troy Maskell has been sentenced to seven years and nine months over the assault death of postmaster John Burke and must spend a minimum of four years and eight months behind bars.Maskell was jailed for eight years in 2023, with a non-parole period of five, after being found guilty of manslaughter in his first trial.LIVE UPDATES: Trump warns the 'big wave' yet to come in war with IranThe 47-year-old looked straight ahead as Victorian Supreme Court Justice James Elliott handed down his revised jail term today.Justice Elliott said his decision was based on Maskell's good behaviour and rehabilitation while in prison from the previous sentence.But he said the "unprovoked and gratuitous violence at vulnerable members of our community" must be denounced.He was found guilty of manslaughter for a second time by a Victorian Supreme Court jury in a retrial of his unprovoked attack on Burke.The 73-year-old postmaster lived alone at the back of his Strathmerton post office in northern Victoria and would often pop into the local service station to chat with the attendant.On August 8, 2021, an intoxicated Maskell walked into the store around midnight with his partner and daughter, whom Burke greeted as he chatted to staff.READ MORE: Cyclone threat puts northern Australia on high alertMaskell's partner called Burke a pedophile, before Maskell picked up a one-litre bottle of sports drink and threw it at Burke's head, causing him to fall.He then walked over to Burke and kicked him as he lay on the tiled floor.Burke died in hospital 11 weeks later from a brain injury.His sister Suzanne Heppell recounted how his slow agonising death was a horrific and catastrophic event that damaged her emotionally and psychologically.Burke had been optimistic in the days after the attack, expressing a belief he would be out of hospital in a few days and back running the post office, his sister recounted.But after suffering two strokes in hospital, his health deteriorated to the point where he lost the ability to move, communicate, or recognise anyone and was paralysed down his right side."One minute John was alive, next he was lying paralysed, unable to communicate, locked in his frozen body," Heppell said."I see Strathy being likened to a jigsaw, with John being a significant piece in that jigsaw. That piece was taken away from this very small, tight community."Maskell successfully appealed his original convictions when a Victorian Court of Appeal board found there had been a substantial miscarriage of justice and ordered a fresh trial in July 2025.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

ai_summary: Troy Maskell sentenced to seven years
Maskell found guilty of manslaughter
Postmaster John Burke killed in attack
Regional Victorian community affected
Maskell sentenced after retrial
